title: Women’s Day Celebration 2026 Theme “Nari in Saree"
text: The Women’s Day Celebration 2026 was successfully organized by Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management and Information Technology (BVIMIT) in collaboration with Apollo Hospitals, Belapur. The event featured a Walkathon to promote women’s health, awareness, and empowerment. The theme of the celebration was “Nari in Saree,” highlighting the grace, strength, and cultural identity of women. The program was jointly organized by the BVIMIT Women Development Cell, DLLE Committee, and ISR Committee. Many students from the institute actively participated in the event with great enthusiasm, especially embracing the theme by wearing sarees during the Walkathon. The event aimed to spread awareness about women’s well-being, equality, and respect in society. Student volunteers also took up important social responsibilities in organizing and managing the event smoothly, demonstrating teamwork, leadership, and community engagement. The celebration was a meaningful initiative that encouraged appreciation of women’s empowerment along with cultural pride. Women Development Cell, DLLE Committee, ISR Committee Dr. Suhasini Vijay Kumar(Principal), Dr Pratibha Deshmukh, Ms.Nidhi , Dr Sambhu Sankar Rai

title: Shivaji Maharaj Jayanti Celebration – 24 February 2026
text: BVIMIT celebrated the birth anniversary of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj on 24th February 2026 with great enthusiasm. The program began with garlanding of the portrait by the campus director Dr V.J.Kadam & Principal. Students and staff attended in traditional attire, adding cultural spirit to the event. The highlights included an energetic Powada, a vibrant Lezim performance, Singing and a speech depicting the life and bravery of Shivaji Maharaj. The celebration concluded with a vote of thanks and the patriotic slogan — “जय शिवराय!”

title: Students Participated in (Extension Activity-Street Play & Poster Making, Creative Writing Competition) UDAAN 2026
text: We are proud to share that the students of Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management and Information Technology (IMIT), Navi Mumbai actively participated in “UDAAN – The Flight of Extension 2026”. Organized by the Department of Lifelong Learning and Extension (DLLE), University of Mumbai in collaboration with Veena College of Commerce and Science dated on 11th February 2026. UDAAN is a prestigious extension initiative aimed at promoting social responsibility, community engagement, and holistic student development. The event provided our students with an enriching platform to showcase their talents, exchange innovative ideas, and contribute meaningfully to extension activities aligned with societal development. Participation in such University-level events reflects IMIT’s commitment to nurturing socially responsible leaders and encouraging experiential learning beyond the classroom. We congratulate our students for their enthusiastic involvement and commend the University of Mumbai and the organizing team for hosting such an inspiring initiative.

title: AI for Atmanirbhar Bharat
text: The Institution’s Innovation Council (IIC–NSEC) of BVIMIT organized a demonstration of student-led AI projects on 2nd February 2026 under the MIC-driven initiative “AI for Atmanirbhar Bharat.” Students showcased AI-based projects addressing real-world problems in healthcare, agriculture, education, and smart governance. The activity promoted innovation, practical learning, and indigenous solution development aligned with the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat. Faculty interaction and feedback encouraged students to pursue research, startups, and responsible use of AI.

title: Vande Mataram Geet Singing Ceremony
text: A Vande Mataram Geet Singing Ceremony was organized at Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management and Information Technology, CBD Belapur on 7th November 2025 at 10:30 a.m. A total of 135 students and 11 staff members enthusiastically participated in the event. The ceremony fostered a sense of patriotism and unity among participants as they collectively rendered the national song with great pride and devotion.

title: DIWALI CELEBRATION 2025
text: The Diwali Celebration was held on 17th October 2025 with the theme “Let’s Celebrate the Festival of Lights with Joy, Unity & Smiles! The event brought together students and staff to celebrate the spirit of togetherness and happiness. The venue was beautifully decorated with diyas, rangoli, and colorful Flowers, creating a festive atmosphere. The celebration began with a warm welcome and Diwali greetings, followed by fun-filled games, music, and cultural performances that added excitement and laughter. Various competitions and activities encouraged enthusiastic participation from both students and faculty. Winners were rewarded with prizes, and the event concluded with a joyful group photo session capturing the unity and festive spirit of the occasion. Overall, the Diwali celebration was a grand success — spreading light, joy, and togetherness among everyone present.

title: AARAMBH 25
text: The Fresher’s Party “Aarambh 2025” was organized on 13th October 2025 at the BVIMIT by the MCA Second Year students under the guidance of Prof. Shubhangi Mahadik & Dr. Rasika Patil, the faculty coordinator. The event aimed to welcome the newly admitted MCA First Year students and help them interact with their seniors and faculty in a friendly atmosphere. The program began with a warm welcome speech. Students showcased their talents through energetic dance performances, melodious singing, skits, and fun games. The decoration team beautifully designed the venue with the theme “Aarambh – A New Beginning”, adding a vibrant touch to the celebration. The highlight of the event was the selection of Mr. Fresher and Ms. Fresher, awarded to the most confident and talented newcomers. The success of the program was the result of excellent teamwork by the decoration, gift, technical, anchoring, and organizing committees. The event concluded with a heartfelt vote of thanks, appreciating everyone’s efforts. Overall, Aarambh 2025 was a grand success that created wonderful memories and marked a cheerful beginning for the new MCA batch at BVIMIT.

title: SHARTOSTAV 2025
text: The Department of MCA successfully organized SHARTOSTAV 2025, a week-long celebration from 23rd to 29th September 2025. The event aimed to foster cultural awareness, encourage creativity, and provide a platform for students to engage in fun and interactive activities. Event Summary: Date Theme Highlights 23rd Sept 2025 Mismatch Day Students creatively wore mismatched outfits. Participation was high, and photo sessions added a fun element. 24th Sept 2025 Twins Day / Group Alike Day Students paired up or formed groups to dress alike. 15 groups participated, showcasing teamwork and coordination. 25th Sept 2025 Fusion Day (BOLLYWOOD) Students blended traditional and modern styles inspired by Bollywood. Music, mini performances, and costume displays made the day vibrant. 26th Sept 2025 Dandiya and Garba Evening Traditional attire was worn, and students actively participated in Dandiya and Garba dances. Around 70 students joined the evening celebration. 29th Sept 2025 Traditional Day Students represented their regional cultures through attire. Cultural presentations highlighted the diversity of traditions. SHARTOSTAV 2025 was a grand success, with enthusiastic participation from students and faculty. The event not only celebrated cultural diversity but also encouraged creativity, collaboration, and community spirit among the students. The Department of MCA thanks all students, faculty members, and organizing committee members for their dedication and support in making SHARTOSTAV 2025 a memorable and engaging celebration.

title: Teachers’ Day Celebration
text: BVIMIT MCA Semester III students celebrated Teachers’ Day with great enthusiasm on 8th September 2025. The program began with a warm welcome and felicitation of faculty members. Students expressed their gratitude through speeches, poems, and fun cultural performances. Various games and activities were also organized to engage teachers and make the occasion memorable. The celebration concluded with a vote of thanks, where students acknowledged the guidance, support, and dedication of their teachers. The event strengthened the bond between teachers and students, making it a joyful and meaningful occasion.

title: Ganesh Festival Celebration
text: On 4th September 2025, the MCA Department of BVIMIT came together to celebrate Ganesh Aarti with great devotion and enthusiasm. Students and faculty participated in the traditional rituals, filling the atmosphere with positivity and cultural spirit. The event highlighted the unity and vibrant energy of the department, as everyone offered prayers to Lord Ganesha for wisdom, success, and prosperity.

title: Ideation Competition
text: The Ideation Competition organized by BVIMIT on 25th August 2025 provided an excellent platform for students to showcase their creativity, innovation, and problem-solving abilities. The event encouraged participants to present unique ideas and practical solutions to real-world challenges across various domains of technology and society. Students enthusiastically pitched their innovative concepts before a panel of judges comprising faculty members and industry experts. The competition fostered teamwork, critical thinking, and entrepreneurial spirit among the participants. Overall, the event was a great success, inspiring young minds to think beyond conventional boundaries and contribute towards innovative future developments.

title: Independence Day on 15 August 2025
text: BVIMIT organized Independence Day on 15 August 2025 with vibrant patriotism and heartfelt enthusiasm. The campus came alive with tricolor decorations, echoing the spirit of freedom and unity. The celebration began with the ceremonial flag hoisting by campus director, followed by the national anthem sung in unison by the Principal, students and faculty. Cultural performances, including patriotic songs, dances, and skits, showcased India’s rich heritage and honored the sacrifices of freedom fighters. The event fostered a sense of pride and togetherness, making it a memorable tribute to India’s journey of freedom.

title: "Say No to Drugs: Drive Session
text: On 14th August 2025, BVIMIT organized a special awareness session on the theme “Say No to Drugs” for MCA students in collaboration with the local police station. The objective of the session was to spread awareness about the harmful effects of drug abuse and to encourage students to adopt a healthy, drug-free lifestyle. Representatives from the police department delivered an impactful talk, sharing real-life incidents, explaining the legal consequences, and highlighting preventive measures to combat substance abuse. The session was interactive, with students actively participating in discussions and seeking clarifications on various aspects of the issue. This initiative reflected BVIMIT’s commitment to fostering social responsibility, moral values, and the overall well-being of its students.

title: Library Activity ‘Read to Lead’ - Book Review Competition
text: ‘Read to Lead’ – Book Review Competition was organized for MCA Semester III Students. Venue: 303 Date: 21.7.2025 Time: 3.15 p.m. Participants: 1. Shraddha Dinesh Chauhan A_10 Book: The Conqueror of Destiny by Raghavan 2. Raj Singh roll no. A_54 Book : The diary of Anny frank by Anny Frank 3. Syed Raquim Hussain Naqvi A_59 Book : Ikigai- writer -Héctor García and Francesc Miralles 4. Atharv Tandel A_60 Book : Rich Dad, Poor Dad - by Robert kiyosaki Winners: Atharv Tandel- 1st Position Syed Raquim Hussain Naqvi - 2nd Position Shraddha Dinesh Chauhan - 3rd Position Raj Singh -Consolidation Prize

title: BVIMIT Convocation Ceremony : Batch 2022-24
text: The Annual Degree Distribution Ceremony for the MCA batch of 2022–24 at Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management & Information Technology was held on 19th July 2025 at the institute’s permanent campus. The event was graced by the presence of the Chief Guest, Mr. Sanjay Sinha, Deputy Director General at the National Informatics Centre. He was warmly felicitated by our Principal, Dr. Suhasini Vijaykumar, who also presented an overview of the institution’s academic achievements and co-curricular progress. The ceremony commenced with the traditional lighting of the lamp by the dignitaries, followed by the conferral of degrees to the top three merit holders: Joisar Kajal Satish, Mandadkar Tejasvini Manohar, and Pant Harshal Devkinandan. In addition, certificates of appreciation were awarded to students who excelled in the subject "Seminar" in Semester IV. The Chief Guest delivered an inspiring address, encouraging graduates to pursue their future careers with passion and commitment. This year, a total of 108 MCA students received their degrees. The ceremony concluded with the national anthem and a group photograph of the graduating batch.

title: Shivaji Maharaj Jayanti 2025
text: Shivaji Maharaj Jayanti is an essential occasion celebrated across Maharashtra and beyond, honoring the birth of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, a great warrior, visionary ruler, and legendary figure in Indian history. He was born in 1630 and is revered for his leadership, courage, and contributions to Indian society and culture. BVIMIT, Navi Mumbai, commemorated the event on 17th March 2025 with a grand and vibrant celebration, also marking Traditional Day. The BVIMIT campus was beautifully decorated in bright Orange vibes, reflecting the traditional essence of the celebration. The event saw the participation of 120 students, who engaged enthusiastically in various activities. Students organized a statue of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj and created a prototype of the culture, showcasing their creativity and imagination. A Pooja and Aarti ceremony was conducted by Dr. Vilasrao Kadam, Director of Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Educational Complex, Navi Mumbai, and Dr. Suhasani VijayKumar, Principal of BVIMIT, Navi Mumbai. All faculty, supporting staff, and students attended the event in traditional Indian attire, creating a festive and unified atmosphere. The entire institute came together for the Pooja, exchanging greetings and sharing in the joyous occasion.

title: Hospital Visit on Women’s Health Awareness
text: Occasion: International Women's Day Celebration Venue: Apollo Hospitals, Navi Mumbai, 11th March 2025 Organized By: Women Development Cell (WDC) Committee collaboration with Apollo Hospitals & Coordinators: • Dr. Pratibha Deshmukh • Ms. Nidhi Speaker: Dr. Bindu K. S., Gynaecologist, Apollo Hospitals The primary objective of this hospital visit was to raise awareness about women's health issues, specifically focusing on breast cancer and cervical cancer. This initiative was part of the International Women's Day celebration, aimed at educating young students about preventive healthcare and empowering them with knowledge about early detection and treatment. The Women’s Health Awareness Visit to Apollo Hospitals was an enlightening and impactful experience. Celebrating International Women’s Day through such initiatives fosters awareness and encourages a healthier future for women in society. The event successfully met its goal of educating and empowering students on key health matters.

title: Report on BVTHON 2025
text: BVTHON 2025, an intra-collegiate hackathon, was successfully organized on 15 feb 2025 with the theme "Tech for Social Good." The event provided a dynamic platform for students to showcase their coding skills and innovative problem-solving abilities, aiming to develop technology-driven solutions for societal challenges. The hackathon witnessed enthusiastic participation from students across various disciplines, who worked tirelessly to create impactful projects addressing real-world issues. Teams collaborated on ideas related to healthcare, education, environmental sustainability, and smart governance, among others. The competition featured multiple rounds, including ideation, prototype development, and final presentations. Expert mentors and industry professionals guided participants, offering valuable insights and feedback. The event concluded with an award ceremony, recognizing the best projects for their creativity, feasibility, and potential social impact. Winning teams were honoured with prizes and certificates, encouraging them to further refine and implement their solutions. BVTHON 2025 successfully fostered innovation, teamwork, and a spirit of social responsibility among students. The event not only highlighted the power of technology in solving social issues but also inspired future technologists to contribute towards a better society. We look forward to even greater participation and groundbreaking ideas in the next edition of BVTHON!

title: Report on Parents –Teacher Meeting AY- 2024-25
text: The Parent-Teacher Meeting (PTM) for MCA I Sem II year students was held on 8th Feb 2025 at 10.30 AM in Classroom 202. The meeting aimed to provide parents with an update on their child's academic progress, discuss areas of improvement, and gather feedback. Attendance: A total of 30 parents attended the meeting, representing 30 students. Agenda: 1. Welcome and Introduction: The coordinator welcomed parents and introduced the faculty members. 2. Academic Progress: Faculty members presented an overview of the students' academic performance, highlighting strengths and weaknesses. 3. Areas of Improvement: Parents were informed about areas where their child needs improvement, and suggestions were provided for supporting their child's learning. 4. Curriculum and Programs: The faculty discussed the MCA curriculum, ongoing projects, and upcoming events. 5. Feedback and Suggestions: Parents were invited to share their feedback and suggestions on how to improve the academic experience for their child. Key Takeaways: - Students demonstrated significant improvement in programming skills and software development. - Parents were advised to encourage and monitor their child to attend value added sessions organized by placement committee. - The faculty emphasized the importance of regular attendance, punctuality, and meeting deadlines of assignment submission. Action Plan: - Parents will receive students regular progress updates via parents group. - Students will participate in various competitions and activities organized by institute. - Faculty members will provide additional support to students who require improvement. Conclusion: The PTM was a successful event, providing parents with valuable insights into their child's academic progress. The faculty looks forward to working closely with parents to ensure the students' continued success. Recommendations for Future PTM’s: - Schedule regular PTM’s to maintain open communication with parents. - More sessions by industry experts to share their experiences and provide guidance to students. - Consider hosting online PTM’s to accommodate parents who cannot attend in-person meetings.

title: Report on Personal Hygiene Session by P&G Hygiene and Gillette India
text: On 16 January 2025 , Bharati Vidyapeeth's Institute of Management & Information Technology hosted a personal hygiene session organized by P&G Hygiene and Gillette India. The session by Mr. Abhishek Choudhary from P&G Hygiene and Gillette India aimed to raise awareness about personal hygiene practices and encourage students and faculty to prioritize their health and well-being. The event was well-received by all attendees, who participated in discussions on various hygiene topics, including daily grooming habits, skincare, and overall cleanliness. P&G Hygiene experts provided valuable insights on maintaining personal hygiene, while Gillette India representatives demonstrated the use of their products. As a part of the session, students and faculty were given hygiene-related goodies. Boys received Gillette Mach 3 razors, and girls were given Whisper sanitary napkins, promoting proper hygiene practices for both genders. The session was an informative and engaging experience that not only educated everyone about hygiene but also provided them with essential products to help maintain a healthy lifestyle. Overall, the event was a success in promoting hygiene awareness, and it reflected the college's commitment to the health and well-being of its community.

title: MIC Programmes & Schemes Dr. Abhay Jere, VCM, AICTE & CIO, MoE
text: On 23.10.2024 a session was organized through online mode on the title “MIC Programmes & Schemes with Dr. Abhay Jere, VCM, AICTE & CIO, MoE” by the MoE Innovation Cell and IIC. The guest speaker was Dr. Abhay Jere, VCM, AICTE & CIO, MoE 11 faculties and 87 students participated in the program. This event was coordinated by Dr. Kirti Muley ,Ms Shubhangi mahadik, IIC Convener, , Assistant professor, BVIMIT College navi mumbai. The guest speaker emphasized that Institution Innovation Council (IIC) at technical institutions should evolve into problem-solving hubs not only for India but for the global community. He stressed the importance of creating awareness on Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) and organizing idea challenges and design thinking workshops to foster creativity among students. In his speech, the speaker highlighted the fact that, despite having more than 1 crore students enrolled across institutions, only 6,000 startups have emerged globally, which signifies a need for improvement in entrepreneurial outcomes. He encouraged students to actively participate in the internal Smart India Hackathon (SIH) and SIH 2024 to develop practical solutions to pressing problems. He also discussed the Yukti Portal, which has seen the submission of over 1 lakh innovative ideas, and urged students to focus on projects at Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 4 for meaningful progress. The guest speaker further mentioned that last year, 130 innovations were funded under the Yukti Innovation Challenge. He highlighted the importance of the newly established Indovation Centres across the country, which provide handholding support for technology transfer. He urged institutions to leverage these centres to enhance their IIC activities. Additionally, Dr. Abhay Jere elaborated on the Kapila Portal, which offers reimbursement for government charges related to IP filing, thereby encouraging students and institutions to protect and promote their innovations through proper intellectual property management.

title: Aamdani Bazar - 2024
text: Bharati Vidyapeeth Institute of Management and Information Technology (BVIMIT) organized “Aamdani Bazar – 2024” on Oct 9th, 2024 at BVIMIT basement. The bazaar focused on how students can learn and exchange at the marketplace. This serves as a roadmap, guiding students to play the role of entrepreneurs in their decision-making processes and earn from their stall(business). The program started at 11:00 am with the ribbon cutting by the hands of Dr. Suhasani VijayKumar Principal BVIMIT Navi Mumbai. MCA Sem - I and II students exhibited their stalls of eatable/non-eatable items/games. There were various stalls like panipuri, chat papadi, taak, bhel puri, various games, etc. Along with the students, faculty members of MCA department also visited the stall and enjoyed eating and playing various games. in this seminar. The bazaar was organized by Start Up Committee under IIC Dr. Pratibha Deshmukh and Ms. Nidhi.

title: Shardotsav – 2024
text: Navratri is a Hindu festival celebrated in the autumn every year. Navratri, in Hinduism, major festival held in honour of the divine feminine. Navratri occurs over 9 days during the month of Ashvin, or Ashvina (in the Gregorian calendar, usually September–October). On this occasion, Bharati Vidyapeeth Institute of Management and Information Technology, Navi Mumbai organized Cultural week “Shardotsav – 2024”. Cultural week “Shardotsav 2024” was celebrated from 4th – 11th October. On Thursday 04-10-24 Retro look \old 80- 90’s event was celebrated. Winner: Boy: Dhananjay Potdar Girl: Khushi Jaiswal Friday 04-10-24 Mehendi Competition and Best Out of Waste was held.Winner: Gayatri Tiker, Khushi Jaiswal Best Out of Waste Winner: Priyanka & Saloni. On Monday 07-10-24 Mismatch Day (Odd costumes) event was celebrated. Winner: Boy: Sahil Patil, Syed Raquim Hussain Naqvi, Anjali singh, Yash patil, Vedant pawar On Monday 07-10-24 Fake Tattoo Day Competition was held. Winner: Khushi R Poojary, Omkaar Pendkalkar Monday 07-10-24 Rangoli Competition was held. Description – Participants made rangoli’s in one hour. Winner: 1st : Sayli Desai 2nd : Siddhi Khose,Akshada Khalate, Snehalata 3rd : Rijuta Tat, Pranjal Phapale. On Tuesday 08-10-24 Cultural day was celebrated. On Tuesday 08-10-24 Speech Competition was held. Winner: 1) Soniya Salunkhe,2)Yash Patil Wednesday 09-10-24 AAMDANNI BAZAAR was held. Participants had put stalls of food and games, and sold their business ideas. Friday 11-10-2024 Traditional Day event was celebrated. 11-10-24 Garba was organized for students of all departments. All the students participated enthusiastically and dressed in Indian traditional attire. They enjoyed the Garba event. The function revealed many aspects of our culture before the audience.

title: AARAMBH 24
text: The Aarambh '24 Freshers Party, held on 30 September 2024, was a lively and memorable event to welcome the MCA batch of 2024-2026. The event, organized by the senior MCA students, featured a mix of cultural performances, interactive games, and a talent show that showcased the creativity of the fresher’s. The fresher’s party aimed to provide the new students with an opportunity to interact with their seniors, faculty members, and peers in a relaxed and fun-filled environment. The highlight of the evening was the Mr. and Ms. Fresher Contest, where students competed in various rounds of talent and personality challenges. Overall, Aarambh '24 was a great success, providing a fun and engaging platform for freshers to integrate into the college community and make lasting memories.

title: MCA SEM I ORIENTATION PROGRAMME REPORT (2024-25)
text: MCA Sem-I orientation programme was organized by Dr. Kirti Muley and Dr. Shambhu Shankar Rai Coordinator MCA sem I, on 19th & 20th Sept. 2024. The purpose behind the orientation programme was to make the students aware about the academics and industry. The programme was inaugurated and welcomed by Dr. Suhasini Vijaykumar, Principal, BVIMIT, Navi Mumbai. The first day of orientation 19th Sept. 2023 has the following sessions: The session 1 stared with industry interaction. Mr. Yogesh Chouksey, UI/UX Designer Mphasis, gave valuable insight in the area of UI/ UX. In second session our esteemed alumni interact with the students and share their overall experience of the college and the corporate life. The session 3 was conducted by Mr. Pradeep Kasbe, Nursing Department, Apolla Hospital, Navi Mumbai, who has given training to the students on First Aid and Basic Life Support. He had given the demonstration on how to safe human life and how to handle the situation during the heart attack. Next session was conducted by Dr. Shalushubham, Physiotherapist Apolla Hospital, Navi Mumbai. She talk about the good posture and ergonomics. She focused how bad postures can affect your overall health. The second day of orientation 20th Sept. 2023 has the following sessions: The session 1 was conducted by Dr. Jyoti Kharade to discuss the newly implemented NEP based syllabus for the MCA 2 year course. In session 2, the panel discussion was also conducted by alumni. Ms. Arati Agrawal, Director, VocSkills Pvt. Ltd, conducted session 3, tell the importance of certification in the field of IT. She also focused on trending technology and prospects in IT industries. The session 4 was conducted by internal faculty members on the topics examination, MOOC, placement, ISTE and IIC. Prof. Shravani Pawar Examination Coordinator, Dr. Priya Chandran ISTE Coordinator, Dr. Pratibha Deshmukh MOOC Coordinator and Dr. Kirti Muley IIC Coordinator addressed the students on various procedures and methodology required for their courses.

title: Parents –Teacher Meeting AY- 2023-24
text: Parent Teachers Meeting for classes MCA Sem 1 and MCA Sem III was conducted on 28th October 2023 from 11.00 AM to 1.00 PM. The parents were intimated through the student official groups as well as parents group well in advance through class teachers and mentors. 25 parents of MCA Sem I and 11 parents of MCA Sem III students attended this meeting. The meeting was addressed by Dr. Suhasini Vijaykumar (Principal of MCA Dept). Wherein importance of student’s attendance in the class was briefly discussed. Parents were also requested to keep in touch with the class coordinators for their wards progress. Interaction between Parents, Class Teachers, Subject Teachers and Mentors was done. There was a discussion on student’s performance in individual subjects, students discipline in classrooms and their attendance percentage. Parents was happy with the way the department was conducting academically and was very satisfied with the performance of their wards. One parent also enquired what kind of placement arrangements are done for their ward. At last, the parents filled the feedback form and expressed their views. Following are the views and suggestion given by parents. Positive Feedback: • Parents praised the faculty and were happy about their wards Progress. • Some Parents were happy with the work of class coordinators and mentors. Suggestions: • Encourage students to become successful entrepreneur and organize the lecture of successful personalities.

title: MCA SEM I ORIENTATION PROGRAMME (2023-24)
text: MCA Sem-I orientation programme was held on 12th & 13th Sept. 2023. The purpose behind the orientation programme was to make the students aware about the academics and industry. The programme was inaugurated and welcomed by Dr. Suhasini Vijaykumar, Principal, Dr. Pratibha Deshmukh and Ms. Gunjan Behl Coordinator MCA sem I, BVIMIT, Navi Mumbai. The programme continued with a warm welcome to the fresher’s and introduced students to Bharati Family. She also gave a brief introduction about the curriculum, the institute, discipline, exams, class code of conduct and all other related issues. The first day of orientation 12th Sept. 2023 has the following sessions: The session 1 stared with industry interaction. Mr. Saurabh Sihna, Founder & Managing Partner REIN Labs & Contexio. interacted with students on recent trends in IT Industries. The session 2 was started with Mr. Kiran Salunkhe and their team delivered a lecture on Human Value. They interacted with the students and spoked on “true gift to the humanity “. The session 3 was conducted by Dr. Hinduja Iruthayarajan, Senior Registrar at Emergency Dept. Apolla Hospital, Navi Mumbai, who has given training to the students on First Aid and Basic Life Support. She had given the demonstration on how to safe human life and how to handle the situation during the heart attack. The second day of orientation 13th Sept. 2023 has the following sessions: The session 1 started with interaction with Placement Officer Prof. Manish Dubey and Prof. Sudeshna Roy. They discussed about BVIMIT placement activity and aptitude and interview preparation methods. The panel discussion was also conducted by alumni and placed students of final year. The session 2 was conducted by internal faculty members on the topics Syllabus discussion, examination, MOOC, ISR and IIC. Dr. Jyoti Kharade Vice Principal, Prof. Shravani Pawar Examination Coordinator, Dr. Shambhu Rai ISR Coordinator, Dr. Pratibha Deshmukh MOOC Coordinator and Dr. Kirti Muley IIC Cooridinator addressed the students on various procedures and methodology required for their courses. During post lunch session 3 Caption Devesh Kumar addressed the students on Personality Development which would be helpful for them in future. The next session 4 was conducted by Ms. Arati Agrawal, Director, VocSkills Pvt. Ltd. delivered on Digital Marketing where students can be benefited from the various channels of social media. She also focused on trending technology and prospects in IT industires. The last session was conducted by Dr. SaluShubham Keni Physiotherapy Specialist Appollo Hospitals, Navi Mumbai, on importance’s of Posture and Ergonomics

title: Elocution Competition Theme: Entrepreneur/ Innovation/ Startup
text: To acknowledge entrepreneurship and individuals becoming entrepreneurs across the world, August 21 is celebrated as 'World Entrepreneurs Day' every year. The day celebrates the role played by businessmen in creating employment opportunities and nurturing economic development across various industries globally. Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management and Information Technology, Navi Mumbai organized Elocution competition. Elocution competition was conducted on 21th August 2023. Students were asked to pick a topic according to theme. Every participant was given precisely 5 minute to prepare the speech on selected topic. It was indeed astounding to see how beautifully students had put their thoughts, insights, humor and delivered the speech of consciousness which was fluent, erudite and articulate while being unscripted. 13 students from MCA Sem 3 participated. The parameter for the judgement was content, fluency, confidence and presentation. The finalists’ displayed oratory skill with zeal and enthusiasm. The competition was culminated with a few words of encouragement by judges and principal. Total Number of Registration: 13 The event was scheduled during 'World Entrepreneurs Day 2023.  On Monday 21-08-2023 Elocution competition contest was held.  Winners: Prathmesh Pradhan  Judges for the event: 1. Dr. Jyoti Kharade 2. Prof. Manish Dubey.

title: Freshers 2023-24
text: Fresher’s party was organized on 30th September 2024 party for the students of MCA AY 2023-25. The event was organized in the Hotel Management Auditorium from 1:00 – 5:00 p.m. The students of second year organized various games and dance performances for the fresher’s. At the Mr. and Miss Fresher’s were declared and were felicitated by the Principal and Faculty Members.

title: Satyanarayan Pooja
text: BVIMIT organized “Satyanarayan Pooja” in college premises on Saturday, 9th September 2023 at 10 a.m. Satyanarayan puja is dedicated to Lord Satyanarayan, one of the form of Lord Vishnu. Satya signifies the truth and Narayan means the higher self. All the staff members & students were celebrated the Satyanarayan Pooja with much excitement and in the end holy Prasad was distributed to all.

title: Teacher’s Day 2023
text: On the occasion of the Birthday of Dr Sarvapalli Radhakrishnan’s Teacher’s day celebration was conducted at Bharti Vidyapeeth campus, Navi Mumbai. To mark the great Teacher & Philosophyist, Dr Sarvapalli Radhakrishnan’s Birthday, Teacher’s Day was observed and celebrated at BVIMIT on September 05, 2023. at Conference Room. All the staff members of MCA department gathered joyfully for the celebration of the event. The Principal Dr. Suhasini VijayKumar enlightened with her speech about the significance of the function to show respect to our Gurus who dispel darkness and ignorance around us. She distributed a flower and token of gift to all the teaching staff members.

title: International Women’s Day 2023
text: Bharati Vidyapeeth's Institute of Management & Information Technology (BVIMIT) Women’s Development Cell on occasion of International Women's Day conducted session on “Free Health Check-up camp” on 8th March 2023 for Teaching and non-teaching staff in association with Apollo Hospital Navi Mumbai. In the camp RBS (Random Blood Sugar), Blood PRESSURE, BMD (Bone Mineral density), BCA and doctor consultation was organized. Dr. Suhasini Vijaykumar Principal, BVIMIT has delivered the speech on women’s day and theme of speech was gender equality and equity. title: Green Beauty competition
text: The National Youth Festival in India is an annual gathering of youth with various competitive and collaborative activities. Celebrated to commemorate the birth anniversary of youth icon Swami Vivekananda, it is organized by Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India in collaboration with one of the State Governments. Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management and Information Technology, Navi Mumbai organized Green Beauty On Monday 10-01-2023 in BVIMIT On the occasion of the Birthday of our Founder Honourable Dr. Patangrao Kadam & National Youth Day Green Beauty competition to spread awareness amongst the students about the various issues those are being faced by our planet regarding our environment and to foster an eco-friendly environment in the college campus. Total Number of Registration: 20 groups.  Winners: • 1st The Squad (Jagjit Kaur, Shreya Nikam, Namrata Gaikwad, Kajal Singh, Esha Sakpal) • 2nd Tag Coders (Uday Gavada, Chinmay Warang, Tejal Sankpal, Tabassum Shaikh, Omkar Dahifale) • 3rdPanvel Gang (Rupali Dandge, Vaishnavi Thavai, Athulya kanayikaran, Abhishek Chandran, Aditi Jambekar)  Judges for the event: 1. Dr. Priya Chandran 2. Dr. Jyoti Kharade 3. Prof. Nidhi Poonia 4. Dr. Partibha Deshmukh

title: Business Ideas Poster Making Competition
text: The National Youth Festival in India is an annual gathering of youth with various competitive and collaborative activities. title: Nasha Mukti
text: Bharati Vidyapeeth’s IMIT organized a talk on Nasha Mukti on 14th January 2023 from 10 AM. The main objective of this program is to Gear UP! Destress, Detox, Discover the youth in life. The talk is about how to handle the stress of our day to day life. It also focuses on the importance of the meditation, through which one can detox his/ her life. Dr. Munishree Abhijeet Kumarji and Munishri Jagrit Kumarji were the speakers for the talk, who enlighten the audience with their knowledge and experience.

title: Free health check camp & BLS & First AID training
text: BVIMIT, Navi Mumbai IQAC and Women Development Cell organized free health check camp on Saturday 11th June 2022 in association with Apollo Hospital Navi Mumbai . In the session BLS & First AID training was given to the faculties, non teaching staff and students by Dr. Jaspreet Singh. BLS course trained the participants to promptly recognize several life-threatening emergencies, give high-quality chest compressions, deliver appropriate ventilations and provide early use of an AED. Session highlights was: High-quality CPR for adults, children, and infants, AHA Chain of Survival, specifically the BLS components and important early use of an AED. In the camp RBS (Random Blood Sugar), Blood PRESSURE, BMD(Bone Mineral density), Eye Checkup and doctor consultation was organized. All teaching, non teaching staff and students from bharati navi Mumbai belapur campus took benefit of the camp.

title: Session on “Hemoglobin Prevalence & Knowledge” -
text: Women Development Cell of BVIMIT organized an interactive session on “Hemoglobin Prevalence & Knowledge” on 19-Jul-2019 for female staff members where Mrs. Chitra Polekar, M.Sc Nursing from BV College of Nursing give awareness regarding Anemia in female. Anemia is a condition in which levels of hemoglobin, hematocrit, and erythrocytes fall below the normal range was given. It was discussed how increasing the intake of iron-rich foods (eggs, spinach, artichokes, beans, lean meats, jaggery, peanuts and seafood) and foods rich in cofactors (such as vitamin B6, folic acid, vitamin B12, and vitamin C) important for maintaining normal hemoglobin levels. In the end, iron test was taken by Mrs. Chitra Polekar . She has advised that the normal range for hemoglobin should be maintained in between, 12.0 to 15.5 grams per deciliter for the age group above 35.

title: MANTHAN
text: A two day technical fest “MANTHAN 2018” was conducted for IT and Non IT Events. The annual technical festival is a rendezvous of the students of all IT colleges in and around Mumbai and Navi Mumbai joining together to explore and visualize the plethora of knowledge for creating the splendor in wisdom and excitement, mixing knowledge with fun. This inter-collegiate competition tests the skills and hones the competencies of young techies through various IT-Technical, Indoor and Outdoor Sport events. The event was inaugurated by Dr. V.J Kadam, Campus Director, Bharti Vidyapeeth, Navi Mumbai on 23rd January 2018. About 80 colleges were invited in and around Mumbai and we got a startling response from these colleges. The event was closed on 24th January 2018 with the prize distribution ceremony. The event was financially supported by the different sponsors.

title: MILAN
text: “Milan” our first alumni meet was organized on 3rd March 2018. Along with fun and frolic students learned important lessons of team work, managing people, some leadership qualities etc. To sum up the event was a great success in both essence and spirit.
